examples.de: Wenn Sie Fragen haben, rufen Sie uns an. → Haben Sie Fragen, rufen Sie uns an.
examples.en: If you have questions, call us.

examples.de: Wenn das Wetter schlecht ist, bleiben wir zu Hause. → Ist das Wetter schlecht, bleiben wir zu Hause.
examples.en: If the weather is bad, we stay at home.

common_mistakes: Using 'wenn' and verb-first structure together incorrectly.
Forgetting that the conjugated verb must come at the beginning of the conditional clause.
Confusing this structure with a yes/no question.
Using this structure too often in casual conversation, where it can sound formal.
Forgetting the comma between the conditional clause and the main clause.
english_test: If you need help, call us.
If you have time, visit me.
If the train is late, we wait.
If you want to register, fill out the form.
If the weather is good, we go for a walk.
persian_test: اگر کمک نیاز دارید، با ما تماس بگیرید.
اگر وقت داری، به من سر بزن.
اگر قطار تأخیر داشته باشد، منتظر می‌مانیم.
اگر می‌خواهید ثبت‌نام کنید، فرم را پر کنید.
اگر هوا خوب باشد، پیاده‌روی می‌کنیم.

Unit 3.description: When the subject or object in the main clause is identical with the subject of the dass-clause, German often uses an infinitive clause with 'zu' instead of a dass-clause. However, after verbs of saying, perception, and knowledge, such as 'sagen', 'antworten', 'sehen', 'hören', 'wissen', 'vermuten', and 'zweifeln', an infinitive clause with 'zu' is usually not possible.

examples.de: Der Wirt kann sich nicht vorstellen, den Krimi allein anzuschauen.
examples.en: The landlord cannot imagine watching the crime show alone.

examples.de: Ich bitte ihn, die Rechnung zu bezahlen.
examples.en: I ask him to pay the bill.

examples.de: Er weiß, dass er mit den Stammgästen rechnen kann.
examples.en: He knows that he can count on the regular guests.

common_mistakes: Using 'Infinitiv + zu' even when it is not allowed after verbs of saying, perception, or knowledge.
Forgetting 'zu' before the infinitive.
Using the infinitive structure when the subjects are different and the meaning becomes unclear.
Putting 'zu' in the wrong place with separable verbs, for example 'anzuschauen'.
Overusing dass-clauses where an infinitive clause would sound more natural.
english_test: I hope to finish the work today.
She plans to call her friend.
He asks me to pay the bill.
We cannot imagine living there.
He knows that he is right.
persian_test: امیدوارم امروز کار را تمام کنم.
او برنامه دارد به دوستش زنگ بزند.
او از من می‌خواهد صورت‌حساب را پرداخت کنم.
ما نمی‌توانیم تصور کنیم آنجا زندگی کنیم.
او می‌داند که حق با اوست.

Unit 3.description: Dass-clauses can function as sentence complements. They often replace a noun phrase in the sentence. In this unit, dass-clauses can replace a Nominativergänzung, Akkusativergänzung, or Präpositionalergänzung. With prepositional complements, German often uses a prepositional adverb such as 'dafür' before the dass-clause.

examples.de: Besonders bemerkenswert ist, dass Tatort bei jüngeren Leuten erfolgreich ist.
examples.en: It is especially remarkable that Tatort is successful among younger people.

examples.de: Viele geben zu, dass sie eine Schwäche für Tatort haben.
examples.en: Many admit that they have a weakness for Tatort.

examples.de: Der Wirt sorgt dafür, dass die Gäste ihre Getränke haben.
examples.en: The landlord makes sure that the guests have their drinks.

common_mistakes: Forgetting the placeholder or prepositional adverb, for example 'dafür', when the verb requires a prepositional complement.
Using main-clause word order inside the dass-clause.
Not recognizing that a dass-clause can replace a noun phrase.
Using an infinitive clause where a dass-clause is needed because the subjects are different.
Forgetting the comma before the dass-clause.
english_test: It is surprising that many young people read newspapers.
He admits that he made a mistake.
She makes sure that the guests are comfortable.
It is important that everyone arrives on time.
We believe that the result is correct.
persian_test: تعجب‌آور است که بسیاری از جوانان روزنامه می‌خوانند.
او اعتراف می‌کند که اشتباه کرده است.
او مراقب است که مهمان‌ها راحت باشند.
مهم است که همه به موقع برسند.
ما باور داریم که نتیجه درست است.

Unit 4.description: This topic explains how to express time relationships between actions using temporal connectors. Some connectors show that two actions happen at the same time, such as 'während', 'solange', and 'als'. Others show that actions do not happen at the same time, such as 'bevor/ehe', 'nachdem', and 'sobald'. These connectors introduce subordinate clauses, so the conjugated verb goes to the end.

examples.de: Während sie noch zur Schule gehen, machen Schüler schon Praktika.
examples.en: While they are still going to school, students already do internships.

examples.de: Bevor Tanja sich als Au-pair bewirbt, informiert sie sich über das Gastland.
examples.en: Before Tanja applies as an au pair, she informs herself about the host country.

examples.de: Sobald man 16 Jahre alt ist, kann man einen Freiwilligendienst leisten.
examples.en: As soon as one is 16 years old, one can do voluntary service.

common_mistakes: Forgetting that the verb goes to the end in clauses with temporal connectors.
Confusing 'bevor' and 'nachdem'. 'Bevor' means before another action; 'nachdem' means after another action.
Using 'als' for repeated events. 'Als' is used for one-time events in the past.
Confusing 'während' as a temporal connector with its later contrast meaning.
Using the same tense incorrectly when 'nachdem' shows that one past action happened before another.
english_test: While she is studying, she listens to music.
Before he applies, he collects information.
As soon as we arrive, we call you.
After she finished school, she started training.
When I wanted to send the application, the computer crashed.
persian_test: در حالی که او هنوز مدرسه می‌رود، کارآموزی می‌کند.
قبل از اینکه درخواست بدهیم، درباره شرکت تحقیق می‌کنیم.
به محض اینکه نامه رسید، به تو خبر می‌دهم.
بعد از اینکه او یک سال استراحت کرده بود، تحصیل را شروع کرد.
وقتی می‌خواستم فرم را پر کنم، اینترنت قطع شد.

Unit 4.description: Temporal relationships can be expressed verbally with connectors or nominally with prepositions. The verbal style uses subordinate clauses with connectors such as 'bevor/ehe', 'während/solange', 'sobald', and 'nachdem'. The nominal style uses prepositions such as 'vor + Dativ', 'während + Genitiv', '(gleich) nach + Dativ', and 'nach + Dativ'. Nominal expressions are especially common in written German.

examples.de: Bevor ich mich als Au-pair bewerbe, informiere ich mich über das Gastland.
examples.en: Before I apply as an au pair, I inform myself about the host country.

examples.de: Vor der Bewerbung als Au-pair informiere ich mich über das Gastland.
examples.en: Before the application as an au pair, I inform myself about the host country.

examples.de: Während der Schulzeit machen Schüler schon Praktika.
examples.en: During school time, students already do internships.

common_mistakes: Using a connector structure and a preposition structure at the same time incorrectly.
Forgetting the correct case after temporal prepositions: 'vor + Dativ', 'nach + Dativ', 'während + Genitiv'.
Using a full clause after a preposition instead of a noun phrase.
Forgetting that nominal expressions are more formal and common in written German.
Confusing 'nachdem' as a connector with 'nach + Dativ' as a preposition.
english_test: Before the application, I read about the company.
During school time, many students do internships.
Immediately after receiving the certificate, they apply.
After the end of the course, we take an exam.
Before the interview, she prepares carefully.
persian_test: قبل از درخواست، درباره کشور میزبان اطلاعات جمع‌آوری می‌کنم.
در طول تعطیلات، او یک دوره زبان گذراند.
بلافاصله بعد از دریافت ایمیل، پاسخ دادم.
بعد از پایان سال تحصیلی، آنها سفر کردند.
قبل از شروع کارآموزی، او قرارداد را خواند.

Unit 4.description: Modal adverbs with the suffix '-weise' describe how something is done or express an evaluation. They can be formed from adjectives or nouns. Sometimes a linking element is inserted before '-weise', for example '-er-', '-s-', '-n-', or no extra element. Examples from the unit include 'normalerweise', 'beziehungsweise', 'stellenweise', and 'probeweise'.

examples.de: Normalerweise fahre ich mit dem Zug zur Arbeit.
examples.en: Normally, I go to work by train.

examples.de: Die Straße war stellenweise sehr glatt.
examples.en: The road was very slippery in places.

examples.de: Wir können die Software probeweise benutzen.
examples.en: We can use the software on a trial basis.

common_mistakes: Forgetting that adverbs ending in '-weise' are written as one word.
Using the adjective form instead of the adverb form, for example 'normal' instead of 'normalerweise'.
Forgetting the linking element, for example 'normalerweise' not 'normalweise'.
Confusing 'beziehungsweise' with a simple 'oder'. It often means 'or rather' or clarifies alternatives.
Overusing '-weise' adverbs in speech, where simpler forms may sound more natural.
english_test: Normally, I start work at eight.
The road was icy in places.
We can test the product on a trial basis.
He answered partially correctly.
The two meetings take place weekly or rather monthly.
persian_test: معمولاً صبح‌ها قهوه می‌نوشم.
جاده در بعضی قسمت‌ها بسته بود.
ما می‌توانیم این برنامه را آزمایشی استفاده کنیم.
او تا حدی درست جواب داد.
این کلاس‌ها هفتگی یا به عبارت دقیق‌تر هر دو هفته یک‌بار برگزار می‌شوند.

Unit 5.description: The verb 'lassen' can have several meanings depending on the structure. With an infinitive, it can mean allowing someone to do something, having something done by someone else, expressing that something is possible, or leaving something unchanged. In the Perfekt, 'lassen' as a full verb uses the normal participle 'gelassen', but 'lassen + Infinitiv' forms the Perfekt with a double infinitive: 'machen lassen'.

examples.de: Laura lässt nicht gern Fotos von sich machen.
examples.en: Laura does not like having photos taken of herself.

examples.de: Manche Modezeitschriften lassen nicht-professionelle Models ihre Mode vorführen.
examples.en: Some fashion magazines have non-professional models present their fashion.

examples.de: Laura hat gern Fotos von sich machen lassen.
examples.en: Laura liked having photos taken of herself.

common_mistakes: Confusing 'lassen' as a full verb with 'lassen + Infinitiv'.
Using 'gelassen' in the Perfekt when 'lassen + Infinitiv' requires the double infinitive.
Forgetting the infinitive at the end of the sentence.
Confusing the meanings: allow, have something done, possibility, or leave unchanged.
Translating directly from English and choosing the wrong 'lassen' structure.
english_test: She has her hair cut.
He lets his children play outside.
The problem can be solved.
You can leave the window open.
She had photos taken of herself.
persian_test: او موهایش را کوتاه می‌کند.
او اجازه می‌دهد بچه‌هایش بیرون بازی کنند.
این مشکل قابل حل است.
می‌توانی پنجره را باز بگذاری.
او از خودش عکس گرفت / گذاشت از او عکس بگیرند.

Unit 5.description: Futur II can be used to express assumptions about past events. It is formed with 'werden' + Partizip II + 'haben' or 'sein'. Instead of Futur II, German can also express the same idea with adverbs such as 'wohl' or 'vermutlich'.

examples.de: Da wird sich ihr Leben ziemlich verändert haben.
examples.en: Her life will probably have changed quite a lot.

examples.de: Ihre Freunde werden neidisch gewesen sein.
examples.en: Her friends will probably have been jealous.

examples.de: Er wird den Zug verpasst haben.
examples.en: He will probably have missed the train.

common_mistakes: Using Futur I instead of Futur II for assumptions about the past.
Forgetting 'haben' or 'sein' at the end.
Using the wrong auxiliary: 'haben' vs 'sein'.
Confusing Futur II as only a future tense, although here it expresses a past assumption.
Overusing Futur II in everyday speech where 'wohl' or 'vermutlich' sounds more natural.
english_test: He will probably have forgotten the appointment.
She will probably have arrived already.
They will probably have been tired.
His life will probably have changed.
She will probably have missed the bus.
persian_test: او احتمالاً قرار را فراموش کرده است.
او احتمالاً قبلاً رسیده است.
آنها احتمالاً خسته بوده‌اند.
زندگی او احتمالاً تغییر کرده است.
او احتمالاً اتوبوس را از دست داده است.

Unit 5.description: Some verbs are connected with infinitives without 'zu'. In this unit, the important verbs are 'gehen', 'lernen', 'bleiben', 'sehen', and 'hören'. With 'sehen' and 'hören', the Perfekt is formed with a double infinitive.

examples.de: Sie gehen öfter mal joggen.
examples.en: They often go jogging.

examples.de: Viele Leute lernen Zumba tanzen.
examples.en: Many people learn to dance Zumba.

examples.de: Ich habe ihn laut lachen hören.
examples.en: I heard him laughing loudly.

common_mistakes: Adding 'zu' after these verbs, for example 'joggen zu gehen' in simple verb combinations.
Forgetting the double infinitive in the Perfekt with 'sehen' and 'hören'.
Using the wrong auxiliary in Perfekt, especially with 'gehen' and 'bleiben'.
Confusing these verb combinations with normal infinitive clauses with 'zu'.
Putting the second infinitive in the wrong position.
english_test: They often go jogging.
Many people learn to dance.
I stay standing here.
He saw me standing on one leg.
I heard him laughing loudly.
persian_test: آنها اغلب برای دویدن می‌روند.
افراد زیادی رقص زومبا یاد می‌گیرند.
من همین‌جا ایستاده می‌مانم.
او من را دید که روی یک پا ایستاده بودم.
من شنیدم که او بلند می‌خندید.

Unit 5.topic: Wortbildung: Nominalisierung von Verben mit Nomen/Adverbien
Unit 5.description: In this word formation topic, the infinitive of the verb becomes a neuter noun. A noun or adverb is placed before the verb to form a compound noun. Examples include noun + verb combinations such as 'das Hüftkreisen' and adverb + verb combinations such as 'das Vorwärtshüpfen'.

examples.de: Das Hüftkreisen ist eine gute Übung.
examples.en: Hip circling is a good exercise.

examples.de: Das Schulterzucken kann Unsicherheit ausdrücken.
examples.en: Shoulder shrugging can express uncertainty.

examples.de: Das Rückwärtshüpfen ist schwieriger als das Vorwärtshüpfen.
examples.en: Jumping backward is more difficult than jumping forward.

common_mistakes: Forgetting that the nominalized infinitive is neuter: das Hüftkreisen.
Writing the compound as separate words instead of one noun.
Forgetting capitalization of nominalized verbs.
Using an article with the wrong gender.
Confusing the compound noun with a normal verb phrase.
english_test: Hip circling is useful.
Shoulder shrugging is common.
Head shaking means no.
Jumping forward is easy.
Jumping backward is difficult.
persian_test: چرخاندن لگن مفید است.
بالا انداختن شانه رایج است.
تکان دادن سر یعنی نه.
پریدن به جلو آسان است.
پریدن به عقب سخت است.

Unit 6.description: Konjunktiv II is used to express unreal conditions, wishes, and comparisons. It can refer to the present or the past. For unreal conditions, German often uses 'wenn' + Konjunktiv II. If the sentence is formed without 'wenn', the conjugated verb in Konjunktiv II stands at the beginning. For unreal wishes, words like 'doch', 'nur', or 'bloß' are commonly used. For unreal comparisons, 'als ob' + Konjunktiv II is used; 'ob' can also be omitted in structures like 'als wäre ...'.

examples.de: Wenn Jana die App nicht verwenden würde, würde sie nicht alle Attraktionen sehen.
examples.en: If Jana did not use the app, she would not see all the attractions.

examples.de: Wenn Jana die App nicht verwendet hätte, hätte sie nicht alle Attraktionen gesehen.
examples.en: If Jana had not used the app, she would not have seen all the attractions.

examples.de: Es sieht so aus, als ob die Welle so hoch wie ein Haus wäre.
examples.en: It looks as if the wave were as high as a house.

common_mistakes: Confusing Konjunktiv II Gegenwart with Konjunktiv II Vergangenheit.
Forgetting 'hätte' or 'wäre' + Partizip II for unreal situations in the past.
Using normal indicative forms instead of Konjunktiv II forms.
Forgetting that without 'wenn', the Konjunktiv II verb goes to position 1.
Using 'als ob' with normal word order instead of subordinate clause word order.
Forgetting common original Konjunktiv II forms such as 'wäre', 'hätte', 'könnte', 'müsste', 'wüsste'.
english_test: If I had more time, I would travel more.
If she had used the app, she would have found the museum.
If only I could live in Berlin!
It looks as if he were tired.
If he had been able to call his friend, he would not have got lost.
persian_test: اگر پول بیشتری داشتم، یک آپارتمان بزرگ‌تر می‌خریدم.
اگر زودتر حرکت کرده بودیم، قطار را از دست نمی‌دادیم.
کاش می‌توانستم در کلن بمانم!
طوری به نظر می‌رسد انگار او همه چیز را می‌داند.
اگر او نقشه را دیده بود، راه را گم نمی‌کرد.

Unit 6.description: Some adjectives are used together with fixed prepositions. The preposition determines the case of the following noun or pronoun. In this unit, some adjective-preposition combinations take Akkusativ, for example 'stolz auf', 'bekannt für', 'verliebt in', and 'überrascht über'. Others take Dativ, for example 'interessiert an', 'bekannt bei', 'zufrieden mit', 'verrückt nach', 'begeistert von', and 'nett zu'. Some adjectives, such as 'bekannt', can be used with different prepositions depending on the meaning.

examples.de: Basel ist stolz auf seine Kunstsammlung.
examples.en: Basel is proud of its art collection.

examples.de: Tom ist sehr interessiert an der Schweizer Kultur.
examples.en: Tom is very interested in Swiss culture.

examples.de: Musikliebhaber sind mit dem Angebot an Konzerten sehr zufrieden.
examples.en: Music lovers are very satisfied with the range of concerts.

common_mistakes: Using the wrong preposition after an adjective.
Using the wrong case after the preposition.
Translating directly from English or Persian instead of learning the German adjective-preposition combination.
Confusing combinations with Akkusativ and Dativ, for example 'stolz auf + Akkusativ' but 'zufrieden mit + Dativ'.
Forgetting that some adjectives can take different prepositions depending on meaning, for example 'bekannt für' and 'bekannt bei'.
english_test: He is proud of his work.
She is interested in German culture.
We are satisfied with the result.
Many tourists are surprised by the cultural offer.
He is crazy about this new muesli.
persian_test: او به موفقیتش افتخار می‌کند.
من به تاریخ آلمان علاقه‌مند هستم.
آنها از خدمات راضی هستند.
ما از این نمایشگاه جدید هیجان‌زده‌ایم.
لطفاً با من مهربان باش.

Unit 7.description: Some German nouns are fixed with specific prepositions. These noun-preposition combinations must be learned together because the preposition determines the case that follows. In this unit, the examples include nouns with prepositions followed by Dativ, such as 'das Bedürfnis nach' and 'die Vorstellung von', and nouns with prepositions followed by Akkusativ, such as 'die Erinnerung an' and 'das Verständnis für'.

examples.de: Das Bedürfnis nach sozialer Sicherheit ist groß.
examples.en: The need for social security is great.

examples.de: Die Vorstellung von Verantwortung macht manchen Menschen Angst.
examples.en: The idea of responsibility scares some people.

examples.de: Ich habe kein Verständnis für dieses Verhalten.
examples.en: I have no understanding for this behavior.

common_mistakes: Using the wrong preposition after a noun.
Using the wrong case after the preposition.
Translating directly from English or Persian instead of learning the German noun-preposition combination.
Confusing noun-preposition combinations with verb-preposition or adjective-preposition combinations.
Forgetting that some nouns require Dativ after the preposition, while others require Akkusativ.
english_test: The need for financial security is increasing.
The idea of a long relationship makes him nervous.
She has a strong memory of her childhood.
Many people have no understanding for this decision.
His fear of responsibility is obvious.
persian_test: نیاز به حمایت اجتماعی زیاد است.
تصور زندگی مشترک برای او سخت است.
او خاطره خوبی از آن سفر دارد.
من برای این وضعیت درک زیادی ندارم.
ترس از آینده برای بسیاری از جوانان طبیعی است.

Unit 7.description: Indirect speech is used to report what someone has said. It is common in news, reports, and formal writing. In indirect speech, German usually uses Konjunktiv I, but Konjunktiv II is often used when the Konjunktiv I form is identical or too similar to the indicative. For the past, indirect speech has only one past form, built with 'haben' or 'sein' in Konjunktiv I or II plus Partizip II.

examples.de: Lucy sagt, Jo sei ihre jüngere Schwester.
examples.en: Lucy says that Jo is her younger sister.

examples.de: Sie sagt, sie habe ihn verlassen.
examples.en: She says that she left him.

examples.de: Die Leute sagten, sie hätten das nicht verstanden.
examples.en: The people said that they had not understood that.

common_mistakes: Using normal indicative forms instead of Konjunktiv I or Konjunktiv II.
Forgetting to change pronouns from direct to indirect speech.
Using too many different past tenses, although indirect speech has only one past form.
Confusing Konjunktiv I and Konjunktiv II forms.
Forgetting 'haben' or 'sein' plus Partizip II for past indirect speech.
english_test: She says that he is her brother.
He says that he has no time.
They say that they did not understand the question.
She said that she had flown to Berlin.
He says that he will come later.
persian_test: او می‌گوید که خواهر کوچک‌ترش اینجاست.
او می‌گوید که او را ترک کرده است.
آنها می‌گویند که موضوع را نفهمیده‌اند.
او گفت که به هواپیما سوار شده است.
او می‌گوید که بعداً برمی‌گردد.

Unit 7.description: Generalizing relative clauses use 'wer', 'wen', or 'wem' to express generally valid statements. The following main clause often begins with a demonstrative pronoun such as 'der', 'die', or 'das'. If the relative pronoun and demonstrative pronoun have the same case, the demonstrative pronoun can sometimes be omitted.

examples.de: Wer sich nicht trennen will, der kann zu einer Verlängerung der Ehe ja sagen.
examples.en: Whoever does not want to separate can say yes to extending the marriage.

examples.de: Wem das Eheleben nicht gefällt, der könnte mit der Ehe auf Zeit glücklich werden.
examples.en: Whoever does not like married life could become happy with a temporary marriage.

examples.de: Wer viel übt, lernt schneller.
examples.en: Whoever practices a lot learns faster.

common_mistakes: Using a normal relative pronoun instead of 'wer', 'wen', or 'wem'.
Choosing the wrong case: 'wer' for nominative, 'wen' for accusative, 'wem' for dative.
Forgetting the demonstrative pronoun in the main clause when it is needed.
Using this structure for one specific person instead of a general statement.
Forgetting verb-final word order in the relative clause.
english_test: Whoever works hard can succeed.
Whoever needs help should call us.
Whoever does not understand the rule can ask.
Whoever I meet today will receive the message.
Whoever this topic does not interest can leave.
persian_test: هر کسی زیاد تمرین کند، سریع‌تر پیشرفت می‌کند.
هر کسی کمک نیاز دارد، باید با ما تماس بگیرد.
هر کسی قانون را نمی‌فهمد، می‌تواند سؤال کند.
هر کسی را امروز ببینم، پیام را دریافت می‌کند.
هر کسی به این موضوع علاقه ندارد، می‌تواند برود.

Unit 7.description: Comparison clauses with 'je ... desto' or 'je ... umso' compare two statements and show a proportional relationship. The first clause begins with 'je' plus comparative. The second clause begins with 'desto' or 'umso' plus comparative. If there is no adjective, 'mehr' is used as the comparative.

examples.de: Je entspannter sie miteinander umgehen, umso wohler fühlen sie sich.
examples.en: The more relaxed they are with each other, the more comfortable they feel.

examples.de: Je mehr sie mit ihrem Partner telefonieren, desto besser kennen sie sich.
examples.en: The more they talk on the phone with their partner, the better they know each other.

examples.de: Je länger man zusammenlebt, desto besser versteht man den anderen.
examples.en: The longer one lives together, the better one understands the other person.

common_mistakes: Forgetting the comparative after 'je' and 'desto/umso'.
Using normal adjective forms instead of comparative forms.
Wrong word order in the 'je' clause.
Forgetting the second part 'desto' or 'umso'.
Using 'viel' instead of 'mehr' when there is no adjective.
english_test: The more relaxed people are, the better they communicate.
The more often they talk, the better they know each other.
The longer they live together, the stronger the relationship becomes.
The less time they have, the more stressful the situation becomes.
The more carefully you listen, the more you understand.
persian_test: هرچه آرام‌تر با هم رفتار کنند، احساس راحتی بیشتری می‌کنند.
هرچه بیشتر با هم صحبت کنند، همدیگر را بهتر می‌شناسند.
هرچه طولانی‌تر با هم زندگی کنند، رابطه قوی‌تر می‌شود.
هرچه وقت کمتری داشته باشند، وضعیت پراسترس‌تر می‌شود.
هرچه با دقت بیشتری گوش کنی، بیشتر می‌فهمی.

Unit 8.description: The modal verb 'sollen' can have a subjective meaning. In this use, it reports or quotes information that one has heard or read. The speaker does not present the information as their own confirmed fact, but as reported information. It can refer to the present or the past. For the past, it is formed with 'sollen' + Partizip II + haben/sein.

examples.de: Mittlerweile soll jeder Deutsche 88,2 kg Fleisch im Jahr verzehren.
examples.en: According to reports, every German is now said to consume 88.2 kg of meat per year.

examples.de: 42 Millionen Menschen sollen diesen Ernährungsstil übernommen haben.
examples.en: It is said that 42 million people have adopted this diet.

examples.de: Der neue Bericht soll sehr zuverlässig sein.
examples.en: The new report is said to be very reliable.

common_mistakes: Confusing subjective 'sollen' with obligation meaning.
Translating 'sollen' only as 'should' instead of recognizing the reported-speech meaning.
Forgetting the double infinitive-like structure in the past: 'sollen ... übernommen haben'.
Using subjective 'sollen' as if the speaker personally confirms the information.
Forgetting that this structure is common in reports, news, and formal written German.
english_test: According to the study, Germans are said to eat less meat.
Many people are said to have changed their diet.
The product is said to be very healthy.
The company is said to have improved the recipe.
The new method is said to be more effective.
persian_test: طبق گزارش، مردم کمتر گوشت مصرف می‌کنند.
گفته می‌شود بسیاری از افراد سبک غذایی خود را تغییر داده‌اند.
گفته می‌شود این محصول بسیار سالم است.
گفته می‌شود شرکت دستور تهیه را بهتر کرده است.
گفته می‌شود روش جدید مؤثرتر است.

Unit 8.description: Nouns can be derived from verbs in different ways. In this unit, nominalization happens through endings such as '-er', '-e', and '-ung', through the verb stem, or through the prefix 'Ge-'. The gender of the resulting noun depends on the formation pattern and must be learned together with the noun.

examples.de: Aus dem Verb 'erzeugen' wird das Nomen 'der Erzeuger'.
examples.en: The verb 'erzeugen' becomes the noun 'der Erzeuger'.

examples.de: Aus dem Verb 'anbauen' wird das Nomen 'der Anbau'.
examples.en: The verb 'anbauen' becomes the noun 'der Anbau'.

examples.de: Aus dem Verb 'zubereiten' wird das Nomen 'die Zubereitung'.
examples.en: The verb 'zubereiten' becomes the noun 'die Zubereitung'.

common_mistakes: Assuming all nominalized verbs use the same ending.
Using the wrong article for the noun.
Confusing different nominalization patterns such as '-ung' and verb stem forms.
Forgetting that nouns formed with '-ung' are usually feminine.
Not learning the noun together with its article and meaning.
english_test: The producer sells regional food.
The cultivation of vegetables is important.
The taste of the drink is unusual.
The harvest begins in September.
The preparation of the meal takes one hour.
persian_test: تولیدکننده محصولات محلی می‌فروشد.
کشت سبزیجات مهم است.
طعم نوشیدنی غیرمعمول است.
برداشت محصول در سپتامبر شروع می‌شود.
آماده‌سازی غذا یک ساعت طول می‌کشد.

Unit 8.description: Conditional relationships express conditions. They can be formed verbally with connectors such as 'wenn', 'falls', and 'sofern', or nominally with the preposition 'bei + Dativ'. Nominal expressions with prepositions are typical of written German.

examples.de: Wenn man ein Produkt teuer verkauft, muss das Etikett stimmen.
examples.en: If a product is sold at a high price, the label must be correct.

examples.de: Falls man reklamiert, schicken viele Firmen Gratisprodukte.
examples.en: If one complains, many companies send free products.

examples.de: Bei einer Reklamation schicken viele Firmen Gratisprodukte.
examples.en: In the case of a complaint, many companies send free products.

common_mistakes: Using main-clause word order after 'wenn', 'falls', or 'sofern'. The verb must go to the end.
Confusing 'wenn' and 'falls'; 'falls' often sounds more uncertain or conditional.
Forgetting that 'bei' requires Dativ.
Using a full clause after 'bei' instead of a noun phrase.
Overusing verbal structures in formal writing where nominal structures would sound more academic.
english_test: If the product is expensive, the quality must be good.
If customers complain, the company sends a replacement.
If you are interested, you will receive more information.
In the case of a complaint, contact customer service.
If the label is wrong, the product cannot be sold.
persian_test: اگر محصول گران باشد، کیفیت باید خوب باشد.
اگر مشتریان شکایت کنند، شرکت جایگزین می‌فرستد.
اگر علاقه‌مند باشید، اطلاعات بیشتری دریافت می‌کنید.
در صورت شکایت، با خدمات مشتری تماس بگیرید.
اگر برچسب اشتباه باشد، محصول نمی‌تواند فروخته شود.

Unit 8.description: Concessive relationships express contrast or contradiction: something happens despite another fact. They can be formed verbally with connectors such as 'obwohl', 'selbst wenn / auch wenn', and 'trotzdem / dennoch', or nominally with prepositions such as 'trotz + Genitiv' and 'selbst/auch bei + Dativ'. Nominal expressions are typical of written German.

examples.de: Bei Reis unterscheiden sich die Haltbarkeitsdaten, obwohl die Qualität gleich ist.
examples.en: With rice, the expiration dates differ although the quality is the same.

examples.de: Viele werfen Obst weg, selbst wenn es nur kleine Makel aufweist.
examples.en: Many people throw fruit away even if it only has small defects.

examples.de: Bei Reis unterscheiden sich die Haltbarkeitsdaten trotz gleicher Qualität sehr stark.
examples.en: With rice, the expiration dates differ greatly despite the same quality.

common_mistakes: Confusing concessive meaning with causal meaning.
Using wrong word order after 'obwohl' or 'selbst wenn'.
Forgetting that 'trotz' is normally used with Genitiv in formal German.
Using a full clause after 'trotz' instead of a noun phrase.
Forgetting that 'trotzdem' and 'dennoch' connect main clauses and require verb position 2.
english_test: Although the quality is the same, the dates are different.
Even if the fruit has small defects, it can still be eaten.
Despite the same quality, the prices differ.
Many products are still usable; nevertheless, they are thrown away.
Even with small defects, many people throw fruit away.
persian_test: با اینکه کیفیت یکسان است، تاریخ‌ها متفاوت هستند.
حتی اگر میوه ایرادهای کوچک داشته باشد، هنوز قابل خوردن است.
با وجود کیفیت یکسان، قیمت‌ها متفاوت هستند.
بسیاری از محصولات هنوز قابل استفاده‌اند؛ با این حال دور ریخته می‌شوند.
حتی با ایرادهای کوچک، بسیاری از مردم میوه را دور می‌ریزند.

Unit 9.description: Konsekutive Zusammenhänge express the consequence or result of a situation or action. They can be expressed verbally with connectors such as 'sodass', 'so ... dass', 'derartig ... dass', 'folglich', and 'infolgedessen', or nominally with the preposition 'infolge + Genitiv'. Nominal expressions with prepositions are typical of written German.

examples.de: Sophie hat ihren Bachelor fast abgeschlossen, sodass sie jetzt Zeit für ein Auslandssemester hat.
examples.en: Sophie has almost completed her bachelor's degree, so she now has time for a semester abroad.

examples.de: Pias Interesse an kulturellen Dingen ist so groß, dass sie gern in einer Großstadt studieren möchte.
examples.en: Pia's interest in cultural things is so great that she would like to study in a big city.

examples.de: Infolge ihres Bachelorabschlusses hat Sophie jetzt Zeit für ein Auslandssemester.
examples.en: As a result of completing her bachelor's degree, Sophie now has time for a semester abroad.

common_mistakes: Using main-clause word order after 'sodass'. The verb must go to the end.
Forgetting that 'so' or 'derartig' comes before an adjective or adverb in 'so/derartig ... dass'.
Confusing consequence with cause; consecutive structures express the result, not the reason.
Using the wrong case after 'infolge'; it requires Genitiv.
Using a full clause after 'infolge' instead of a noun phrase.
english_test: She studied a lot, so she passed the exam.
His interest is so strong that he wants to study abroad.
The course was so difficult that many students failed.
As a result of the accident, the train was delayed.
He had little experience; consequently, he made mistakes.
persian_test: او زیاد درس خواند، به طوری که در امتحان قبول شد.
علاقه او آنقدر زیاد است که می‌خواهد خارج از کشور تحصیل کند.
دوره آنقدر سخت بود که بسیاری از دانشجوها قبول نشدند.
در نتیجه تصادف، قطار تأخیر داشت.
او تجربه کمی داشت؛ در نتیجه اشتباه کرد.

Unit 9.topic: Feste Verbindung von Nomen mit Verben
Unit 9.description: Fixed noun-verb combinations are expressions in which a noun and a verb appear together as a set phrase. They are frequent in written German. In spoken German, a simple verb with the same or similar meaning is often used instead. Examples from the unit include 'eine Lösung finden', 'eine Entscheidung treffen', 'eine Frage stellen', 'Verantwortung übernehmen', 'über Kenntnisse verfügen', and 'einen Vortrag halten'.

examples.de: Wir müssen schnell eine Lösung finden.
examples.en: We have to find a solution quickly.

examples.de: Die Regierung hat eine wichtige Entscheidung getroffen.
examples.en: The government made an important decision.

examples.de: Sie verfügt über gute Deutschkenntnisse.
examples.en: She has good German skills.

common_mistakes: Using the wrong verb with the noun, for example saying 'eine Entscheidung machen' instead of 'eine Entscheidung treffen'.
Translating directly from English or Persian instead of learning the fixed German combination.
Forgetting the correct article of the noun inside the expression.
Confusing formal noun-verb combinations with simpler spoken verbs.
Not recognizing that some nouns can combine with several verbs, for example 'eine Entscheidung treffen' or 'eine Entscheidung fällen'.
english_test: We need to find a solution.
She made a decision yesterday.
He asked an important question.
They take responsibility for the project.
The professor gave a lecture.
persian_test: ما باید یک راه‌حل پیدا کنیم.
او دیروز تصمیم مهمی گرفت.
او یک سؤال مهم مطرح کرد.
آنها مسئولیت پروژه را بر عهده می‌گیرند.
استاد یک سخنرانی ارائه داد.

Unit 9.topic: Wortbildung: Negation durch Vor- und Nachsilben bei Adjektiven
Unit 9.description: German can form negative adjectives with prefixes and suffixes. In this unit, the negative prefixes are 'a-', 'des-', 'ir-', 'miss-', 'non-', and 'un-'. The negative suffix shown is '-los'. These forms help create adjectives such as 'asozial', 'desillusioniert', 'irrelevant', 'missverständlich', 'nonverbal', 'uninteressant', and 'anspruchslos'.

examples.de: Diese Information ist irrelevant.
examples.en: This information is irrelevant.

examples.de: Der Text ist missverständlich formuliert.
examples.en: The text is phrased ambiguously.

examples.de: Die Aufgabe war anspruchslos.
examples.en: The task was undemanding.

common_mistakes: Using the wrong negative prefix because the choice often depends on the word and must be learned.
Assuming that all adjectives can take 'un-' for negation.
Confusing similar forms such as 'unverständlich' and 'missverständlich'.
Forgetting that '-los' often means 'without' or 'lacking something'.
Misspelling prefixes such as 'ir-' or 'des-'.
english_test: This argument is irrelevant.
His behavior was antisocial.
The explanation was misleading or ambiguous.
The communication was nonverbal.
The task was undemanding.
persian_test: این استدلال نامربوط است.
رفتار او ضداجتماعی بود.
توضیح گمراه‌کننده یا مبهم بود.
ارتباط غیرکلامی بود.
وظیفه بی‌زحمت و ساده بود.

Unit 10.topic: Alternativen zum Passiv: Adjektive auf -bar und -lich
Unit 10.description: Many adjectives ending in '-bar' or '-lich' are derived from verbs and can function as alternatives to passive structures. Adjectives ending in '-bar' usually mean that something can be done. Adjectives ending in '-lich' sometimes have a similar passive meaning. The negation of these adjectives is formed with the prefix 'un-'.

examples.de: Das ist ein realisierbares Projekt.
examples.en: That is a project that can be realized.

examples.de: Die Ware ist lieferbar.
examples.en: The goods can be delivered / are available for delivery.

examples.de: Dieser Text ist verständlich.
examples.en: This text can be understood / is understandable.

common_mistakes: Using '-bar' and '-lich' as if they were always interchangeable.
Forgetting that '-bar' usually means 'can be done', for example 'lieferbar' = can be delivered.
Forgetting the negative prefix 'un-', for example 'unerreichbar' or 'unverständlich'.
Using a full passive sentence when a shorter adjective form would be more natural.
Forgetting adjective endings when the adjective comes before a noun.
english_test: This goal cannot be reached.
The product can be delivered tomorrow.
The text is understandable.
This person cannot be replaced.
The project can be realized.
persian_test: این هدف دست‌نیافتنی است.
این کالا فردا قابل تحویل است.
متن قابل فهم است.
این انسان جایگزین‌ناپذیر است.
این پروژه قابل اجرا است.

Unit 10.topic: Alternativen zum Passiv: sich lassen + Infinitiv / sein + zu + Infinitiv
Unit 10.description: Active sentences with 'sich lassen + Infinitiv' and 'sein + zu + Infinitiv' can replace passive sentences with modal meanings such as 'können', 'müssen', 'sollen', or 'dürfen'. 'Sich lassen + Infinitiv' often expresses possibility. 'Sein + zu + Infinitiv' can express necessity, obligation, possibility, or prohibition depending on the context.

examples.de: Ein 3-Gänge-Menü lässt sich für 10 Euro machen.
examples.en: A three-course menu can be made for 10 euros.

examples.de: Die Rechnung ist noch zu bezahlen.
examples.en: The bill still has to be paid.

examples.de: Das Restaurant ist nicht zu verkaufen.
examples.en: The restaurant cannot / must not be sold.

common_mistakes: Forgetting 'sich' in the structure 'sich lassen + Infinitiv'.
Forgetting 'zu' in the structure 'sein + zu + Infinitiv'.
Confusing the meaning of 'sein + zu + Infinitiv': it can mean must, should, can, or may, depending on context.
Using 'werden' unnecessarily when the passive alternative is intended.
Putting the infinitive in the wrong position.
english_test: The menu can be prepared for 10 euros.
The bill still has to be paid.
The restaurant cannot be sold.
This problem can be solved quickly.
The form must be filled out today.
persian_test: این منو را می‌توان با ده یورو آماده کرد.
صورت‌حساب هنوز باید پرداخت شود.
رستوران قابل فروش نیست.
این مشکل سریع قابل حل است.
فرم باید امروز پر شود.

Unit 10.description: In passive sentences, the accusative object of the active sentence becomes the nominative subject of the passive sentence. However, if the active sentence has no accusative object, the passive sentence cannot have a real subject. If position 1 is empty, 'es' is used in position 1 as a placeholder.

examples.de: Mit den Vorbereitungen wird schon im Februar begonnen.
examples.en: Preparations are already started in February.

examples.de: Es wird mit den Vorbereitungen schon im Februar begonnen.
examples.en: Preparations are already started in February.

examples.de: Es muss im Sommer bis in den Abend gearbeitet werden.
examples.en: Work has to be done until evening in summer.

common_mistakes: Trying to create a nominative subject when the active sentence has no accusative object.
Forgetting 'es' in position 1 when no other element occupies position 1.
Thinking that 'es' is a real subject; here it is only a placeholder.
Confusing subjectless passive with normal passive where an accusative object becomes the subject.
Using passive forms with verbs that do not fit the intended meaning.
english_test: Preparations are started in February.
Work must be done until evening in summer.
Deliveries are made daily.
People dance at the festival.
There is a lot of discussion during the meeting.
persian_test: آماده‌سازی از فوریه شروع می‌شود.
در تابستان باید تا عصر کار شود.
روزانه تحویل داده می‌شود.
در جشن زیاد رقصیده می‌شود.
در جلسه زیاد بحث می‌شود.

Unit 11.description: Indefinitpronomen are used when we talk or write about people or things that are unknown, indefinite, or not specified in detail. In this unit, the forms include 'man', '(irgend)jemand', 'niemand', '(irgend)etwas', 'nichts', '(irgend)einer/-e/-s', '(irgend)welche', and 'keiner/-e/-s'. Except in nominative, many indefinite pronouns are declined like articles. With '(irgend)jemand' and 'niemand', the accusative and dative endings can often be omitted in modern usage.

examples.de: Wenn ihr irgendjemanden aus unserem Kurs seht, gebt Bescheid.
examples.en: If you see anyone from our course, let us know.

examples.de: Ich habe niemanden im Büro gesehen.
examples.en: I did not see anyone in the office.

examples.de: Kann mir jemand helfen?
examples.en: Can someone help me?

common_mistakes: Using the wrong case form, for example 'jemand' instead of 'jemanden' in accusative.
Confusing 'man' with 'Mann'. 'man' means 'one / people in general', while 'der Mann' means 'the man'.
Using 'nicht jemand' instead of 'niemand'.
Forgetting that '(irgend)einer/-e/-s' and 'keiner/-e/-s' must match gender, number, and case.
Using 'nichts' for people. 'Nichts' refers to things, not people.
english_test: Someone called you yesterday.
I did not see anyone at the station.
One should not smoke here.
Did you buy anything?
None of the participants answered.
persian_test: دیروز کسی با تو تماس گرفت.
من هیچ‌کس را در ایستگاه ندیدم.
اینجا نباید سیگار کشید.
چیزی خریدی؟
هیچ‌کدام از شرکت‌کنندگان جواب ندادند.

Unit 11.description: Modal relationships express the way, method, or manner in which something happens or is done. They can be formed verbally with connectors such as 'dadurch, dass', 'indem', 'ohne dass', 'ohne ... zu', '(an)statt dass', and '(an)statt ... zu'. They can also be expressed nominally with prepositions such as 'durch + Akkusativ', 'ohne + Akkusativ', and 'statt + Genitiv'. Nominal expressions with prepositions are especially typical of written German. 'Indem' can only be used when the subjects in the main clause and subordinate clause are the same.

examples.de: Man kann sich das Rauchen abgewöhnen, indem man ein spezielles Pflaster verwendet.
examples.en: One can stop smoking by using a special patch.

examples.de: Die Anbieter versprechen schnelle Heilung, ohne dies objektiv zu begründen.
examples.en: The providers promise quick healing without objectively justifying this.

examples.de: Statt der ständigen Arbeit am Computer sollte man sich mehr bewegen.
examples.en: Instead of constantly working at the computer, one should move more.

common_mistakes: Using 'indem' when the subjects in the main clause and subordinate clause are different.
Confusing 'indem' with 'weil'. 'Indem' expresses method, not reason.
Using 'ohne ... zu' or '(an)statt ... zu' when the subjects are different; in that case, 'ohne dass' or '(an)statt dass' is needed.
Forgetting that 'durch' and 'ohne' take Akkusativ.
Forgetting that 'statt' takes Genitiv in formal written German.
Using a full subordinate clause after a preposition instead of a noun phrase.
english_test: One can improve pronunciation by listening every day.
He left without saying goodbye.
Instead of working all evening, she went for a walk.
Through regular practice, you can make progress.
The company promised success without giving a clear reason.
persian_test: می‌توان با گوش دادن روزانه تلفظ را بهتر کرد.
او بدون خداحافظی رفت.
او به جای کار کردن تمام شب، پیاده‌روی کرد.
از طریق تمرین منظم می‌توان پیشرفت کرد.
شرکت بدون ارائه دلیل روشن، وعده موفقیت داد.

Unit 12.description: The extended participle describes a person or thing more precisely, similar to a relative clause. It functions like an adjective and is mostly used in written German. Partizip I has an active meaning and describes an ongoing or non-completed action. Partizip II usually has a completed and often passive meaning.

examples.de: Der ständig telefonierende Junge sitzt im Zug.
examples.en: The boy who is constantly talking on the phone is sitting on the train.

examples.de: Die begeistert applaudierenden Zuschauer standen auf.
examples.en: The spectators who were applauding enthusiastically stood up.

examples.de: Die schon lange bezahlten Rechnungen liegen im Ordner.
examples.en: The invoices that were paid long ago are in the folder.

common_mistakes: Confusing Partizip I active meaning with Partizip II passive or completed meaning.
Forgetting adjective endings after the participle.
Using extended participles too often in spoken German, where relative clauses usually sound more natural.
Placing the extra information in the wrong position before the noun.
Making the phrase too long and difficult to understand.
english_test: The constantly talking boy is annoying.
The enthusiastically applauding audience was happy.
The long-paid bills are in the folder.
The quickly learned language helped her at work.
The carefully prepared presentation was successful.
persian_test: پسری که مدام تلفن می‌زند آزاردهنده است.
تماشاگرانی که با هیجان دست می‌زدند خوشحال بودند.
صورت‌حساب‌هایی که مدت‌ها پیش پرداخت شده‌اند در پوشه هستند.
زبانی که در مدت کوتاهی یاد گرفته شد به او در کار کمک کرد.
ارائه‌ای که با دقت آماده شده بود موفق بود.

Unit 12.description: Adversative clauses express contrast or opposition between two statements. In this unit, the main adversative connectors are 'während', 'dagegen', and 'im Gegensatz dazu'. 'Während' introduces a subordinate clause with the verb at the end. 'Dagegen' and 'im Gegensatz dazu' are used in main clauses and create contrast with the previous statement.

examples.de: Auf dem Land hört man noch Dialekt, während Jugendliche in Städten fast nur Hochdeutsch sprechen.
examples.en: In the countryside people still hear dialect, whereas young people in cities almost only speak standard German.

examples.de: Jugendliche in Städten sprechen fast nur Hochdeutsch. Dagegen hört man auf dem Land noch Dialekt.
examples.en: Young people in cities almost only speak standard German. In contrast, dialect can still be heard in the countryside.

examples.de: In der Stadt spricht man oft Hochdeutsch. Im Gegensatz dazu wird auf dem Land noch Dialekt gesprochen.
examples.en: In the city, standard German is often spoken. In contrast to that, dialect is still spoken in the countryside.

common_mistakes: Using main-clause word order after 'während'.
Confusing temporal 'während' meaning 'while' with adversative 'während' meaning 'whereas'.
Using 'dagegen' as if it introduced a subordinate clause.
Forgetting that 'im Gegensatz dazu' refers back to a previous statement.
Using too many contrast connectors in one sentence.
english_test: In the city people speak standard German, whereas in the village people still speak dialect.
He likes quiet places. In contrast, his sister loves big cities.
Many adults read newspapers. In contrast, young people prefer online news.
She studies during the week, whereas he works.
This method is expensive. In contrast to that, the other method is cheaper.
persian_test: در شهر مردم آلمانی معیار صحبت می‌کنند، در حالی که در روستا هنوز گویش محلی صحبت می‌شود.
او جاهای آرام را دوست دارد. در مقابل، خواهرش شهرهای بزرگ را دوست دارد.
بسیاری از بزرگسالان روزنامه می‌خوانند. در مقابل، جوانان اخبار آنلاین را ترجیح می‌دهند.
او در طول هفته درس می‌خواند، در حالی که او کار می‌کند.
این روش گران است. در مقابل، روش دیگر ارزان‌تر است.

Unit 12.description: Participles can be used as nouns. This allows a short and often gender-neutral way of expression, for example 'Liebe Studierende' instead of 'Liebe Studenten, liebe Studentinnen'. When participles are used as nouns, they are capitalized and declined like adjectives.

examples.de: Liebe Studierende, bitte öffnen Sie das Kursbuch.
examples.en: Dear students, please open the course book.

examples.de: Tanja ist Angestellte in der Stadtverwaltung.
examples.en: Tanja is an employee in the city administration.

examples.de: Für Heranwachsende ist Zweisprachigkeit meist kein Problem.
examples.en: For adolescents, bilingualism is usually not a problem.

common_mistakes: Forgetting to capitalize the participle when it is used as a noun.
Forgetting adjective declension endings.
Using only masculine plural forms when a gender-neutral form would be more appropriate.
Confusing participles used as nouns with normal adjectives.
Using the wrong article or ending, for example 'der Angestellte', 'die Angestellte', 'die Angestellten'.
english_test: Dear students, welcome to the course.
The employee works in the city administration.
All employees can speak with the manager.
Adolescents often learn languages quickly.
The injured person was taken to the hospital.
persian_test: دانشجویان عزیز، به کلاس خوش آمدید.
کارمند در اداره شهر کار می‌کند.
همه کارکنان می‌توانند با مدیر صحبت کنند.
نوجوانان اغلب زبان‌ها را سریع یاد می‌گیرند.
فرد آسیب‌دیده به بیمارستان برده شد.

Unit 12.description: The linking element '-s-' connects the parts of a compound noun. In this unit, it appears after certain suffixes: -heit, -ion, -ität, -keit, -ling, -schaft, -tum, and -ung. Examples include 'Freiheitskampf', 'Diskussionsrunde', 'Identitätsverlust', 'Tätigkeitsbereich', 'Zwillingsbruder', 'Freundschaftspreis', 'Eigentumswohnung', and 'Ankündigungstext'.

examples.de: Der Freiheitskampf dauerte viele Jahre.
examples.en: The struggle for freedom lasted many years.

examples.de: Die Diskussionsrunde beginnt um 18 Uhr.
examples.en: The discussion round begins at 6 p.m.

examples.de: Der Tätigkeitsbereich ist sehr groß.
examples.en: The area of activity is very broad.

common_mistakes: Forgetting the linking '-s-' in compound nouns.
Adding '-s-' where it does not belong.
Writing compound nouns as separate words instead of one word.
Not recognizing that '-s-' often appears after specific suffixes.
Using the wrong article for the final noun in the compound; the final noun determines the gender.
english_test: The struggle for freedom was long.
The discussion round was interesting.
The loss of identity is a serious topic.
The area of activity is clearly defined.
The apartment owned by someone is expensive.
persian_test: مبارزه برای آزادی طولانی بود.
جلسه بحث جالب بود.
از دست دادن هویت موضوعی جدی است.
حوزه فعالیت به‌وضوح تعریف شده است.
آپارتمان ملکی گران است.
